# Swap ETH to BNB
> An independent informational dashboard for comparing non-custodial routes that convert ETH on Ethereum Mainnet into native BNB on BNB Smart Chain.

## What is Swap ETH to BNB
Swapping ETH to BNB is a cross-chain exchange because the source and destination assets exist on separate blockchains. It combines asset conversion with cross-chain transfer, often through a bridge, liquidity network, or intent-based solver. A simple bridge may deliver wrapped ETH on BNB Smart Chain rather than BNB; a complete cross-chain swap delivers the requested destination asset. Unlike a centralized exchange, a non-custodial route requires no exchange deposit account, although users still trust the selected protocol’s contracts and verification model.

## How it works
1. Connect an EVM-compatible wallet, select ETH on Ethereum Mainnet as the input, and select native BNB on BNB Smart Chain as the output.
2. A routing service requests quotes that account for available liquidity, bridge paths, price impact, fees, and minimum output.
3. After reviewing the destination chain, recipient, approvals, and minimum received, the user signs the required source-chain transaction.
4. The selected route swaps and transfers value through bridge contracts or liquidity providers, then releases or purchases BNB on BNB Smart Chain for the recipient.
5. Source and destination transactions can be verified independently using Etherscan and BscScan.

## Key facts
- Ethereum Mainnet uses chain ID 1; BNB Smart Chain Mainnet uses chain ID 56.
- ETH is Ethereum’s native gas asset and is not itself an ERC-20 token.
- BNB is BNB Smart Chain’s native gas asset and is not itself a BEP-20 token.
- WETH and WBNB are tokenized representations; they are not interchangeable with native assets in every application.
- Ethereum and BNB Smart Chain are separate EVM-compatible networks with independent balances and transaction histories.
- A dashboard can remain non-custodial while underlying route contracts temporarily lock, burn, mint, or exchange assets.
- Bridge trust models vary and may rely on validators, oracles, multisignatures, liquidity providers, solvers, or underlying-chain verification.
- Completion time is route-dependent and can increase because of confirmations, finality rules, liquidity, relayers, or network congestion.
- Every valid transaction requires ETH for Ethereum gas; destination gas may be supplied as BNB, deducted from output, or required separately.

## Common questions
- **Is swapping ETH to BNB safe?** Safety depends on the route, contracts, approvals, verification system, and wallet security. Confirm chain 56, native BNB output, minimum received, contract permissions, and recipient before signing.
- **Does the dashboard take custody?** The informational dashboard does not hold private keys or discretionary control over funds. Any executed transfer is governed by the connected wallet and the selected third-party protocol.
- **What fees apply?** Total cost can include Ethereum gas, swap or bridge fees, liquidity-provider or solver charges, price impact, and destination execution costs. Compare final BNB received, not only the displayed exchange rate.
- **How long does it take?** There is no universal settlement time. Check both transaction hashes and the selected provider’s status before retrying a delayed transfer.
- **Which assets and chains are supported?** This topic specifically covers ETH on Ethereum Mainnet and native BNB on BNB Smart Chain; live route availability depends on the provider and amount.
- **How do I start?** Use an EVM wallet containing ETH plus source-chain gas, request a quote, verify the route and recipient, then sign only the transactions you understand.
- **What are the main risks?** Smart-contract exploits, compromised bridge operators, solver failure, bad token contracts, excessive approvals, slippage, phishing, wrong-network transfers, and low-liquidity routes can cause loss.

## App
- Dashboard: https://swap-eth-to-bnb.github.io/
- App: https://swap-eth-to-bnb.github.io/dashboard/

## Official resources
Ethereum Networks: https://ethereum.org/developers/docs/networks/
Ethereum Bridge Documentation: https://ethereum.org/developers/docs/bridges/
BNB Smart Chain Overview: https://docs.bnbchain.org/bnb-smart-chain/overview/
BNB Smart Chain Wallet Configuration: https://docs.bnbchain.org/bnb-smart-chain/developers/wallet-configuration/
BNB Chain Cross-Chain Bridge Guide: https://docs.bnbchain.org/bnb-smart-chain/cross-chain-bridge/
BNB Chain Bridge: https://www.bnbchain.org/en/bnb-chain-bridge
BNB Chain Canonical Bridge Source: https://github.com/bnb-chain/canonical-bridge
Ethereum Official X: https://x.com/ethereum
BNB Chain Official X: https://x.com/BNBCHAIN

## Data & references
Etherscan Ethereum Explorer: https://etherscan.io/
BscScan BNB Smart Chain Explorer: https://bscscan.com/
CoinGecko Ethereum: https://www.coingecko.com/en/coins/ethereum
CoinGecko BNB: https://www.coingecko.com/en/coins/bnb
DeFiLlama Bridge Data: https://defillama.com/bridges
ERC-20 Standard: https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-20
Ethereum Mainnet Chain ID Standard: https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-2228
